entities.name: Corselets
entities.description: An ancient Danish charm of neck-laces, composed of thunderstones engraved with magical letters to resist noxious influences and lightning.

entities.name: Spirits of Saturn
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Saturn, appearing like old men with prominent beards, or as serpents, cats, wolves, bad/badgers, and panthers.

entities.name: Spirits of Jupiter
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Jupiter, appearing like glorious kings with many attendants.

entities.name: Spirits of Mars
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Mars, appearing with weapons brandishing and shining armour, often as lions, wolves, tigers, or bears.

entities.name: Spirits of Sol (Sun)
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Sun, appearing as castles, gardens, mountains, rivers, etc.

entities.name: Spirits of Venus
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Venus, appearing as courtiers, ladies, princes, queens, infants, and children.

entities.name: Spirits of Mercury
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Mercury, appearing as horsemen, fishers, laborers, priests, students, servants, and monstrous animals.

entities.name: Spirits of Luna (Moon)
entities.description: Spirits under the influence of Luna, appearing as ghosts and shadows, sometimes in human shape.

entities.name: Agrippa
entities.description: A cited author/magician whose experiences and declarations are used to validate certain magical effects.

claims: experience with fumes of Man's Gall and black Cat eyes

entities.name: Tying of the Point
entities.description: A knot or ligature used to restrain sexual acts between married persons.

attributes.status: prohibited by laws in several nations

entities.name: Transplantation
entities.description: The magical transferring of diseases from one person to another.

mechanism: burying certain images in neighbours ground
attributes.nature: considered more natural than conjuration by the author

entities.name: Sympathy and Antipathy
entities.description: The principle that things are drawn to their like or repelled by their contrary.

mechanism: In verbis, herbis & lapidibus latet virtus

entities.name: Critique of Conjuration
entities.description: The author's argument that many magicians are actually in compact with the Devil and that their 'charms' are merely disguises for demonic contracts.

attributes.core_argument: Characters and sigils are themselves compacts disguised by strange repetitions.

summary: This passage from Reginald Scot's 'Discoverie of Witchcraft' details the specific planetary correspondences used in magical fumigations, describing the appearances and required ingredients for spirits under Saturn, Jupiter, Mars, Sol, Venus, Mercury, and Luna. The text also explores various charms, such as pentacles and telescopes, while providing a skeptical critique that many conjuration rituals are actually deceptive compacts with the Devil.
